result:
## Summary

Diagnosed the site as **mid-pivot**: content already reads "Lumina" (AI meeting assistant), but the underlying code still had four concrete SEO bugs — cross-referenced against GSC data, which showed zero ranking presence for anything meeting/Lumina-related and all traffic sitting on legacy email-cleanup terms buried at position 40–60.

**Fixed in source** (`app/`):
1. **`layout.tsx`** — the default page metadata (title, description, OG/Twitter tags) still said *"InboxZap — Clean Your Inbox in Minutes"*, completely mismatched to the actual Lumina content. Since the homepage had no metadata override, this was what Google would show in search results and social shares. Replaced with correct Lumina branding, added `metadataBase`/canonical, and site-wide `Organization` + `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
2. **`app/blog/page.tsx`** — the blog index was a client-only component fetching `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist (404). It rendered nothing but a permanent loading spinner to crawlers, orphaning all 12 blog posts from internal linking — the likely root cause of their terrible average position despite real impression volume. Converted to a server component with real `<a href>` links to each post.
3. **`app/sitemap.ts`** — hardcoded to 4 static URLs with a TODO stubbing out blog posts, despite those posts being indexed and getting GSC impressions. Now generates entries for all 12 posts from a shared data source.
4. **`app/page.tsx`** — nav/footer link to `/faq`, which 301-redirects to `/#faq` (per `next.config.js`, "folded into the homepage during the redesign") — but no `id="faq"` section existed anywhere, so every FAQ click/crawl hit a dead end. Added a real FAQ section with `FAQPage` JSON-LD (also useful for AI Overviews/PAA visibility).

Verified all four with a local production build (`next build` + `next start`) — confirmed correct metadata, working redirect, 12 crawlable blog links, and a complete sitemap.

**Not fixed, worth flagging:**
- `/img/og-lumina.png` is referenced but doesn't exist in `public/` — social share previews are broken.
- The 12 blog posts themselves are 100% legacy InboxZap content (bulk-delete-emails, Gmail storage, etc.) with no posts targeting meeting-assistant/transcription keywords — a content-strategy gap beyond this session's editable scope (no `[slug]` route exists in source to add new posts against).
- `next@14.2.5` has a flagged security vulnerability (unrelated to SEO, but `npm install` surfaced it).
